additionalLabels.description: Allows specifying custom labels for DaemonSet of `network-latency-exporter`.

affinity.description: The pod's scheduling constraints. More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.30/#affinity-v1-core

annotations.description: Annotations is an unstructured key value map stored with a resource that may be set by external tools to store and retrieve arbitrary metadata

checkTarget.description: The comma-separated list of network protocols and ports (separated by ':') via which packets will be sent. Supported protocols: UDP, TCP, ICMP. If no port specified for protocol, port 1 will be used

createGrafanaDashboards.description: Allow creating Grafana Dashboards for network-latency-exporter

discoverEnable.description: Allow enabling/disabling script for discovering nodes IP

extraArgs.description: Extra arguments to pass to the network-latency-exporter

image.description:
Network latency exporter image to deploy.
Default: ghcr.io/netcracker/qubership-network-latency-exporter:main

labels.description: Map of string keys and values that can be used to organize and categorize (scope and select) objects

latencyTypes.description: The comma-separated list of collectors. Supported latency types: node_collector

name.description: Provide a name in place of network-latency-exporter for labels

nodeSelector.description: Allow to define which Nodes the Pods are scheduled on

packetSize.description: The size of packet to sent in bytes

packetsNum.description: The number of packets to send per probe

createClusterRole.description: Allow creating ClusterRole. If set to false, ClusterRole must be created manually

createClusterRoleBinding.description: Allow creating ClusterRoleBinding. If set to false, ClusterRoleBinding must be created manually

privileged.description: If true, set parameters in PSP or SCC for ability to running container in the privileged mode and set privileged: true to the security context in the exporter's container

setupSecurityContext.description: Allow creating PodSecurityPolicy or SecurityContextConstraints. If set to false, PodSecurityPolicy / SecurityContextConstraints must be created manually

requestTimeout.description: The response time for each packet sent which the application waits response, in seconds

resources.description: The resources describes the compute resource requests and limits for single Pods. Ref: https://kubernetes.io/docs/user-guide/compute-resources/

limits.description: Limits describe the maximum amount of compute resources allowed

cpu.description: The maximum amount of CPU allowed. Ref: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/configuration/manage-resources-containers/#meaning-of-cpu

memory.description: The maximum amount of memory allowed. Ref: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/configuration/manage-resources-containers/#meaning-of-memory

requests.description: Requests describe the minimum amount of compute resources required

cpu.description: The minimum amount of CPU required. Ref: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/configuration/manage-resources-containers/#meaning-of-cpu

memory.description: The minimum amount of memory required. Ref: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/configuration/manage-resources-containers/#meaning-of-memory

securityContext.description: SecurityContext holds pod-level security attributes. The parameters are required if a Pod Security Policy is enabled for Kubernetes cluster and required if a Security Context Constraints is enabled for OpenShift cluster

runAsUser.description:
The UID to run the entrypoint of the container process. Defaults to user specified in image metadata if unspecified. The default value is set to "0",
 because otherwise the exporter does not work correctly.

fsGroup.description: A special supplemental group that applies to all containers in a pod. If unset, the Kubelet will not modify the ownership and permissions of any volume.

serviceAccount.description: Service account for network-latency-exporter to use. Ref: https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/configure-pod-container/configure-service-account/

create.description: Allow creating ServiceAccount. If set to false, ServiceAccount must be created manually

name.description: Provide a name in place of network-latency-exporter for ServiceAccount

enabled.description: If true, a ServiceMonitor is created for a prometheus-operator

timeout.description: The metrics collection timeout. Can be calculated as TIMEOUT = 10s + (REQUEST_TIMEOUT * PACKETS_NUM * <NUMBER_OF_PROTOCOLS>)

tolerations.description: Tolerations allow the pods to schedule onto nodes with matching taints. Default tolerations "operator: Exists" for installing on master nodes
